sections.paragraph:
συγχαίρω impf. συνέχαιρον; fut. συγχαρήσομαι and 3 sg. συγχαρεῖται Gen 21:6; 2 aor. pass. (with same sense as act. and mid.) συνεχάρην (Aeschyl., X. et al.; ins, pap, LXX)

① to experience joy in conjunction w. someone, rejoice with τινί someone (Aristot., EN 1166a, 8; UPZ 148, 3 [II b.c.]; BGU 1080, 2; Diogenes, Ep. 16 p. 108, 22 Malherbe; Philo, Det. Pot. Ins. 124) Lk 1:58; Phil 2:17f (s. also 2 below); ITr 1:1. συνεχάρην ὑμῖν μεγάλως I rejoiced with you from the bottom of my heart Pol 1:1. τινί foll. by ὅτι rejoice w. someone because (PLond I, 43, 3f p. 48 [II b.c.]) Lk 15:6, 9. Without dat., which is easily supplied (X., Hiero 5, 4; SIG 1173, 5 [138 a.d.]) 1 Cor 12:26 (metaph.: the ‘parts’ stand for the believers).—W. dat. of thing as cause of rejoicing (Herm. Wr. 1, 26.—In such case the compound prob. has the same mng. as the simple verb, as Jos., Ant. 15, 210 [opp. ἄχθεσθαι]) οὐ χαίρει ἐπὶ τῇ ἀδικίᾳ συγχαίρει δὲ τῇ ἀληθείᾳ it does not rejoice over injustice, but rejoices in the truth 1 Cor 13:6 (EFranz, TLZ 87, ’62, 795–98). Cp. Hs 8, 2, 7.

② to express pleasure over another’s good fortune, congratulate τινί someone (Aeschin. 2, 45; Socrates, Ep. 33, 2 [=p. 300, 15 Malherbe] w. ὅτι foll.; Polyb. 15, 5, 13; 30, 18, 1 al.; Diod S 22, 13, 7; Plut., Mor. 231b; PTebt 424, 5; cp. Jos., Ant. 8, 50) B 1:3; IEph 9:2; IPhld 10:1; ISm 11:2; Hs 5, 2, 6.—Lk 1:58 and Phil 2:17f could perh. be classed here as well.—M-M. s.v. συνχαίρω. TW.

impf. impf. = imperfect

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

act. act. = active

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

Diogenes Diogenes = letters attributed to the philosopher D. of Sinope; date uncertain—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

Malherbe Malherbe = AM., various works

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

Pol Pol = Polycarp to the Philippians—List 1

foll. foll. = followed, following

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

dat. dat. = dative

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

metaph. metaph. = metaphor(ically)

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

prob. prob. = probable, probably

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

opp. opp. = opposed to, opposite

TLZ TLZ = Theolog. Literaturzeitung—List 6

Hs Hs = Similitudes

Aeschin Aeschin , IV b.c.; spurious letters II a.d.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

IPhld IPhld = Ignatius to the Philadelphians—List 1

ISm ISm = Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans, I–II a.d.—List 1

perh. perh. = perhaps

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 953
